generated_comment: Ayyo Karthik, don't cry for this type of girl. From the beginning itself I told you, she was too friendly with everyone and always wanting attention, these are not good signs. Some girls know how to pull a man in and then go wherever it suits them; next time choose a simple, steady girl who knows the value of one relationship.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes from one woman to 'some girls' as manipulative seductresses and implies women who are sociable or attention-seeking are inherently untrustworthy. It encodes homewrecker/seductress framing in a maternal, comforting tone rather than through explicit hostility.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 52. Married for 28 years. A homemaker in Chennai. Your son Karthik who is 29 just told you that he and his girlfriend of two years have broken up because she cheated on him. You are sitting with Karthik in the living room. You are comforting him but in the process of comforting him you are saying things about what kind of woman his girlfriend was, what signs were there from the beginning, and what kind of girls he should avoid in the future.
